---
title: "Roystonea"
author: "Jorge Alonso"
date: "2026-05-30"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    self_contained: true
---

# 1. Preparing for analysis

## 1.1. Loading data and packages

```{r}
# Loading packages
library(tidyverse)
library(DHARMa)
library(car)
library(lmtest)
library(MASS)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(performance)
```

```{r}
# Limpeza do histórico e da memória
rm(list=ls(all=TRUE)) 
gc() 
```

```{r}
# Database link
processed_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jmalonso55/Applied-Statistics-for-a-Tropical-Arboretum/refs/heads/main/data/processed_data.csv"
```


```{r}
# Loading data
data_trab <- read.csv(processed_url, sep = ",")

# Checking
summary(data_trab)
```

## 1.2. Filtering database

```{r}
# Filtering for Arecaceae
palmeira <- data_trab %>% 
  filter(family == "Arecaceae")
```


```{r}
# Filtering for Roystonea
roystonea <- palmeira %>% 
  filter(genus == "Roystonea", !is.na(species))
```


```{r}
# Identificar canteiros com mais de 3 indivíduos
canteiros_n3 <- roystonea %>%
  count(sec_cant) %>%
  filter(n > 3) %>%
  pull(sec_cant)

# Base filtrada
roystonea_n3 <- roystonea %>%
  filter(sec_cant %in% canteiros_n3) %>% 
  mutate(species == factor(species), sec_cant = factor(sec_cant))
```


```{r}
ggplot(roystonea_n3, aes(x = dap, y = altura, color = species)) +
  geom_point()
```

# 2. Regression analysis

## 2.1. Fixed effects

### 2.1.1. Linear model

```{r}
# Linear model
mod1 <- lm(altura ~ dap + species, data = roystonea_n3)
```

```{r}
# Analysis of residuals
res_mod1 <- simulateResiduals(mod1, n = 1000)
plot(res_mod1)
```

### 2.1.2. Log model

```{r}
# Log model
mod_log <- lm(altura ~ log(dap) + species, data = roystonea_n3)
```

```{r}
# Analysis of residuals
res_mod_log <- simulateResiduals(mod_log, n = 1000)
plot(res_mod_log)
```

### 2.1.3. Quadratic model

```{r}
# Quadratic model
mod2 <- lm(altura ~ dap + I(dap^2) + species, data = roystonea_n3)
```


```{r}
# Analysis of residuals
res_mod2 <- simulateResiduals(mod2, n = 1000)
plot(res_mod2)
```

```{r}
# ANOVA for comparing linear and quadratic regression models
anova(mod1, mod2)
```

```{r}
# Quadratic model summary
summary(mod2)
```

## 2.2. Mixed effects model

### 2.1.1. Linear model

```{r}
# Mixed effects linear model
mod1m <- lmer(altura ~ dap + species + (1 | sec_cant), data = roystonea_n3, REML = F)
```


```{r}
# Analysis of residuals
res_mod1m <- simulateResiduals(mod1m, n = 1000)
plot(res_mod1m)
```

### 2.1.2. Quadratic model

```{r}
# Mixed effects quadratic model
mod2m <- lmer(altura ~ dap + I(dap^2) + species + (1 | sec_cant), data = roystonea_n3, REML = F, control = lmerControl(check.scaleX = "ignore"))
```


```{r}
# Analysis of residuals
res_mod2m <- simulateResiduals(mod2m, n = 1000)
plot(res_mod2m)
testUniformity(res_mod2m)
testDispersion(res_mod2m)
testOutliers(res_mod2m)
```

```{r}
# Understanding the residues

## Residuals versus fitted values
plotResiduals(res_mod2m, roystonea_n3$dap)

## Residuals versus fixed effects
plotResiduals(res_mod2m, roystonea_n3$especie)

## Residuals versus random effects
plotResiduals(res_mod2m, roystonea_n3$sec_cant)
```

### 2.1.3. Comparing models

```{r}
# ANOVA for comparing regression models
anova(mod1m, mod2m)
```

```{r}
# AIC and BIC of fixed and mixed linear and quadratic models
AIC(mod1, mod2, mod1m, mod2m)
BIC(mod1, mod2, mod1m, mod2m)
```


### 2.1.4. Filtering influent observations

```{r}
## Model diagnostics
roystonea_diag <- roystonea_n3 %>%
  mutate(fitted = fitted(mod1m),
    resid = residuals(mod1m),
    resid_stud = rstudent(mod1m),
    cooks = cooks.distance(mod1m),
    leverage = hatvalues(mod1m),
    out_resid = abs(resid_stud) > 3,
    out_cook = cooks > 0.20,
    out_influente = out_resid | out_cook)

## Possibly influent observations
roystonea_influentes <- roystonea_diag %>%
  filter(out_influente) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(resid_stud))) %>%
  dplyr::select(tombo, altura, dap, fitted, resid, resid_stud, cooks, leverage, out_resid, out_cook)

## Filtered database
roystonea_filtrada <- roystonea_diag %>%
  filter(!out_influente)
```

### 2.1.5. Readjusting the quadratic model

```{r}
# Modelo quadrático misto
mod2m_f <- lmer(altura ~ dap + I(dap^2) + species + (1 | sec_cant), data = roystonea_filtrada, REML = T, control = lmerControl(check.scaleX = "ignore"))
```


```{r}
# Analysis of residuals
res_mod2m_f <- simulateResiduals(mod2m_f, n = 1000)
plot(res_mod2m_f)
testUniformity(res_mod2m_f)
testDispersion(res_mod2m_f)
testOutliers(res_mod2m_f)
```

```{r}
# Model summary
summary(mod2m_f)
```

```{r}
# Model ANOVA
anova(mod2m_f)
```


```{r}
# Variance explained by random effects
icc(mod2m_f)
```


```{r}
# Model adjusted R²
r2(mod2m_f)
```


```{r}
# Means comparison between species
emm_sp <- emmeans(mod2m_f, ~ species)

multcomp::cld(emm_sp, adjust = "sidak", Letters = letters)
```

# 3. Results visualization

```{r}
# Sequência de DAP e níveis de species/canteiro para predição
dap_seq <- seq(
  min(roystonea_filtrada$dap, na.rm = TRUE),
  max(roystonea_filtrada$dap, na.rm = TRUE),
  length.out = 300
)

species_seq <- unique(roystonea_filtrada$species)
cant_seq <- unique(roystonea_filtrada$sec_cant)
```


```{r}
# Curvas médias globais por species: apenas efeitos fixos
pred_global <- expand.grid(
  dap = dap_seq,
  species = species_seq
) %>%
  mutate(
    altura_pred = predict(
      mod2m_f,
      newdata = .,
      re.form = NA
    )
  )
```


```{r}
# Gráfico das curvas médias globais por species
ggplot(roystonea_filtrada, aes(x = dap, y = altura)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = species), alpha = 0.45) +
  geom_line(
    data = pred_global,
    aes(
      x = dap,
      y = altura_pred,
      color = species
    ),
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "DAP (cm)",
    y = "Altura (m)",
    color = "Species"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```


```{r}
# Efeitos aleatórios dos canteiros
efeito_cant <- ranef(mod2m_f)$sec_cant %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("sec_cant") %>%
  rename(efeito_canteiro = `(Intercept)`)
```


```{r}
# Curva média global, marginalizando species
pred_global <- expand.grid(
  dap = dap_seq,
  species = unique(roystonea_filtrada$species)
) %>%
  mutate(
    altura_pred = predict(
      mod2m_f,
      newdata = .,
      re.form = NA
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(dap) %>%
  summarise(
    altura_pred = mean(altura_pred, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```


```{r}
# Curvas preditas por canteiro, marginalizando species
pred_cant <- expand.grid(
  dap = dap_seq,
  sec_cant = unique(roystonea_filtrada$sec_cant),
  species = unique(roystonea_filtrada$species)
) %>%
  mutate(
    altura_pred = predict(
      mod2m_f,
      newdata = .,
      re.form = NULL
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(dap, sec_cant) %>%
  summarise(
    altura_pred = mean(altura_pred, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(efeito_cant, by = "sec_cant")
```


```{r}
# Paleta ordenada pelo valor do efeito aleatório
efeito_ord <- pred_cant %>%
  distinct(sec_cant, efeito_canteiro) %>%
  arrange(efeito_canteiro)

cores_cant <- colorRampPalette(
  c("red", "orange", "yellow", "cyan", "blue")
)(nrow(efeito_ord))

names(cores_cant) <- efeito_ord$sec_cant

labels_cant <- paste0(
  efeito_ord$sec_cant,
  " (", round(efeito_ord$efeito_canteiro, 2), ")"
)

names(labels_cant) <- efeito_ord$sec_cant
```


```{r}
# Gráfico com pontos por species, curvas por canteiro e curva média global
ggplot(roystonea_filtrada, aes(x = dap, y = altura)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = species), alpha = 0.45) +
  geom_line(
    data = pred_cant,
    aes(
      x = dap,
      y = altura_pred,
      group = sec_cant,
      color = sec_cant
    ),
    linewidth = 0.6,
    alpha = 0.80
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = pred_global,
    aes(x = dap, y = altura_pred),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 1.3,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = cores_cant,
    breaks = efeito_ord$sec_cant,
    labels = labels_cant,
    name = "Canteiro\n(efeito aleatório)"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "DAP (cm)",
    y = "Altura (m)",
    shape = "Species"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```
